scaled_oracle_cases.description: Combined test for missing cell, whitespace trimming, charge validation, and dedup in sequences_mapping.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that mapRow handles missing cells (row shorter than header), trims whitespace from peptide_name and charge_state, validates charge as integer defaulting to '0' on invalid, and prevents duplicate entries in sequences_mapping when called repeatedly.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Function must: (1) not raise IndexError for rows with fewer cells than header indices; treat missing as empty string. (2) strip whitespace from peptide_name and charge_state. (3) if charge_state after stripping is not a valid integer (including 'NA' or empty), default to '0'. (4) avoid adding duplicate keys to sequences_mapping list for the same peptide.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Seed gold solution would raise IndexError on missing cell, preserve whitespace in keys, keep invalid charge as literal string, and add duplicate keys in sequences_mapping on repeated calls.
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
mapRow(row1, header_dict, precursors_mapping, sequences_mapping, protein_mapping)
mapRow(row1, header_dict, precursors_mapping, sequences_mapping, protein_mapping)
mapRow(row2, header_dict, precursors_mapping, sequences_mapping, protein_mapping)
print('Precursors Mapping:', precursors_mapping)
print('Sequences Mapping:', sequences_mapping)
print('Protein Mapping:', protein_mapping)
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include tests with rows missing optional columns.
Scaled executable cases must test trimming and charge validation.
Scaled executable cases must include repeated row processing to test dedup.
